questions.q: What is the rated power capacity of this campus solar array?
questions.explanation: The array is rated at 120 kW — enough to power roughly 20 average U.S. homes.

questions.q: How many tonnes of CO₂ does this solar array offset each year?
questions.explanation: 85 tonnes of CO₂ avoided annually — equivalent to taking about 18 cars off the road.

questions.q: Which layer of a solar panel converts sunlight directly into electricity?
questions.explanation: The silicon P-N junction is the photovoltaic cell. Photons knock electrons loose, creating a current.

questions.q: What percentage of campus solid waste does this center divert from landfill?
questions.explanation: 60% diversion rate — the national campus average is around 40%, so this exceeds it by 20 points.

questions.q: Which material provides the highest energy savings when recycled instead of virgin production?
questions.explanation: Recycling aluminum saves up to 95% of the energy needed to produce it from bauxite ore.

options: Buying products made from recycled content
Placing non-recyclables in the bin hoping they'll be recycled
questions.explanation: Wish-cycling contaminates entire loads — when in doubt, throw it out (or compost).

questions.q: How many EV charging bays does the campus station provide?
questions.explanation: 8 bays are available 24/7; the station handles both Level 2 AC and DC fast charging.

questions.q: What fraction of the electricity used for charging comes from renewable sources?
questions.explanation: The station is powered entirely by the campus solar array and purchased RECs.

questions.q: Which connector standard do most North American EVs (non-Tesla) use for DC fast charging?
questions.explanation: CCS (SAE Combo) combines AC and DC pins in one plug — now the dominant standard. NACS is Tesla's; J1772 is AC-only.

questions.q: How much organic food does the campus garden harvest each semester?
questions.explanation: 200 kg per semester — all donated to the campus food pantry, cutting food insecurity and transport emissions.

questions.q: Who manages the day-to-day operations of the campus garden?
questions.explanation: A student-run crew of volunteers plants, tends, and harvests — earning service-learning credit.

questions.q: What is the primary benefit of composting kitchen and garden waste on-site?
options: Returns nutrients to the soil, reducing need for synthetic fertiliser
questions.explanation: On-site composting closes the nutrient loop: food scraps become rich humus that feeds next season's plants.
